channel.title: Careers on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Careers on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Scenes From Unretirement
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about scenes from unretirement.

item.title: Catalyst Team Members
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about catalyst team members.

item.title: Game Development As An Identity
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game development as an identity And

item.title: It's OK To Be Proud
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about why it’s okay for you to be proud, especially of your work and things you do.

item.title: Game Developer Stage Limitations
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game developer stage limitations, which is a somewhat complicated talk.

item.title: My Uncredited Game
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my uncredited game.

item.title: Why People Quit
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about why people quit.

item.title: Recognizing Good Coworkers
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about recognizing good co-workers — and by good I mean good for working with, for you.

item.title: Showcasing Your Work
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about showcasing your work.

item.title: Mentoring
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about mentoring.

item.title: Insecurity
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about insecurity.

item.title: Game Design Portfolio
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about your game design portfolio or, more specifically, what you, as a game designer, should include in a portfolio that you’re sending to a game company when you apply for a job.

item.title: Remote Work
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about remote work and specifically, this is an answer to a question from R3 M1 who asks: hey Tim, what’s your take on remote staff versus having the team work in an office?

item.title: Technology Changes Jobs
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about how technology changes jobs.

item.title: Awards
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Awards, specifically game awards, more specifically, game awards that my games have won and a few that I personally have won.

item.title: Teaching
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about teaching, in terms of what lessons I learned by teaching and kind of how I applied some of those lessons to game development.

item.title: Hiring People
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about hiring people.

item.title: Are You Above Average?
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me Tim. Are you above average? Let’s talk about that today. I want to talk about whether or not you think you are above average at something like: are you good at, really really good at — this thing better than most other people? So let’s set aside impostor syndrome — I have a whole video on that. I’m not talking about that — those times where you feel like, oh no, someone’s GNA find out that I’m terrible and fire me.

item.title: Game Development Roles
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game development roles.

item.title: My Worst Job Interview
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my worst job interview.

item.title: Fallout's Effect On Me
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about fallout’s effect on me, which seems like a very personal, self-reflected, reflective, almost narcissistic topic.

item.title: Lessons By Company
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about what lessons I learned by company.

item.title: Game Development Tunnel Vision
item.description: Sal SE, Tim, par gotcha. I bet you right now some of you are reaching for that YouTube translate button and some of you were thinking, whoa, Tim’s French accent is terrible, trust me, that’s been said. No, I’m doing this video about game development, tunnel vision, because it’s one of the things that occurred to me. I get a lot of questions about a lot of things and early on, when there were less of you, I would answer and say, oh, that’s not something I know or I can’t talk about it I did do a video on things I can’t or won’t answer, but since then there’s been a lot more questions that Encompass a lot more things, even about game development or tangentially related game development, that I literally cannot answer. I either don’t know it or I’m not qualified to answer.

item.title: Selling A Game Design Spec
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about selling a game design spec.

item.title: Would I Work On Fallout Again?
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to answer the question: would I work on Fallout again?

item.title: So You Want To Make Games?
item.description: It’s me, Tim, so you want to make games today?

item.title: Being Underestimated
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about being underestimated.

item.title: Why I Directed One Last Time
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about why I directed one last time and specifically what I mean by that is why I stepped up to be a game director on The Outer Worlds, even after I said I wi it.

item.title: Snapshots Through My Career
item.description: Hey everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I wanted to do something fun that I’m going to call snapshots through my career, and

item.title: Game Development Contracting
item.description: Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game development Contracting.

item.title: Jobs, Careers, And Callings
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about having a job versus a career, versus a calling.

item.title: Making A Demo
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about making a demo.

item.title: Industry Changes
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about industry changes because there have been a lot of stuff going on: a lot of layoffs recently, a lot of concern about AI taking jobs.

item.title: Career Advice From Adam Savage
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about some career advice from Adam Savage.

item.title: Perseverance
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about perseverance.

item.title: Remembering The Game Developers
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the people in game development and specifically

item.title: Job Interview Tests
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about job interview tests.

item.title: Being Proactive
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about being proactive.

item.title: Scenes From A Life Of Game Development
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to show you scenes from a life spent in game development.

item.title: Sometimes It's Just Business
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about that phrase — nothing personal, it’s just business — which is usually used by companies when they’re about to do something not too nice and they don’t want you to take it personally.

item.title: Good Team Characteristics
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about people I like working with, and

item.title: Job Interview Questions
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about job interview questions.

item.title: My Work Pattern For Designing
item.description: Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my work pattern for Designing, and you can consider this a companion video to the one I did on programming, which I’ll put a link in the description.

item.title: School vs. Self-Taught
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the pros and SC cons of going to school versus being self-taught.

item.title: Programmer Roles Explained
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to answer another question, this one from ATA 94, who asks: can you tell me what is the difference between a lead position and a so-called not lead position?

item.title: School As Preparation For Game Development
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about school as preparation for game development.

item.title: The Decline Of Generalists
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about the decline of generalists, and I’ll describe what that is.

item.title: My Bad Game Development Skills
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about game development tasks that I’m bad at.

item.title: Why I'm Semi-Retired
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I’m going to answer a question from banefane who asks: are you planning to retire from game development?

item.title: Career Summary 1981-2023
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to answer a question from asdfjk1227 who asks: can you do a resume video where you talk about every gaming project you did and then a couple, two short versions of interesting events or something, so we can get a better understanding of your experiences in one spot, like 67, 60 seconds per project or company maximum to get a high level?

item.title: My Memoirs
item.description: Hey everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to revisit something I mentioned in a previous video, where I talked about my notes, and I’ll link that below.

item.title: Why I Won't Run Another Business
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to answer another question, this time from user Delta pi 8859, who asks a large number of questions, luckily about one topic.

item.title: Being A "Celebrity" Game Developer
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about what it’s like to work with other people who know who I am.

item.title: Career Longevity
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about career longevity.

item.title: My Work Pattern For Programming
item.description: Hey everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my work pattern while coding.

item.title: Impostor Syndrome
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about insecurity, or what I mentioned in a previous video about game conferences, that feeling of imposter syndrome.

item.title: Ageism In The Game Industry
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about ageism in the game industry and that’s why I’m wearing a shirt which you may have noticed, is a little tight.

item.title: Challenges Facing Game Programmers
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to continue from yesterday’s video about challenges designers face.

item.title: Being A Gay Game Developer
item.description: Hi, it’s me, Tim. It is June 1st, happy pride month.

item.title: My First Professional Game
item.description: Hi. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the first professional game that I ever worked on.

item.title: How to Get a Job in the Game Industry
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I’m going to answer the most asked question I ever get, not just on this channel but at conventions, by email when people visit the studios I worked at friends, family.

item.title: Semi-Retirement
item.description: Hi, it’s me, Tim.
